legal: BYO-original. This manifest ships METHODS/OFFSETS ONLY and patches the user's OWN, legally-obtained APK in their browser (in RAM). No copyrighted game bytes are distributed.

native.comment: In-place ARM byte-patches on lib/armeabi-v7a/libgame.so. Each patch is verified against `expect` (first bytes at `off`) before writing, so a different/wrong libgame.so aborts loudly. RET_YES = mov r0,#1;bx lr  RET_NO = mov r0,#0;bx lr.

dex.comment: TILT FIX, fully in-browser (no apktool). Applied ATOMICALLY by the Rust→WASM dex_tilt_rewrite (wasm/src/lib.rs): the two same-size byte-patches below (register() always registers; unregister() neutered) PLUS a code-item REWRITE of onSensorChanged(SensorEvent)V — a new, larger code_item (modern landscape mapping + try/catch around the native onSensorChanged(FFFJ) call) is appended to the code section, the method's code_off is repointed to it, every u32 file-offset >= insertion point is shifted, and Adler32/SHA-1 are recomputed. The try/catch swallows the UnsatisfiedLinkError thrown by the early sensor events that fire before libgame.so binds the native method (the minimal byte-only patch crashed there). If the dex isn't the 1.5.2 build (descriptor/offset checks fail) the wasm returns 0 and classes.dex is left untouched — never half-patched. The byte-patch offsets are documented here for the record; the wasm verifies them against `expect` and applies them internally.

tilt.desc: Accelerometer tilt fix for modern Android (2014 game never registers the sensor on Android 13+)

bytePatches.name: register.drop-isEnabled-gate
bytePatches.desc: register() always registers (NOP the isEnabled gate)

bytePatches.name: unregister.neuter
bytePatches.desc: unregister() no-op so the sensor stays on (return-void; nop)

androidManifest.comment: Privacy cull, fully in-browser (no apktool). The patcher edits the binary AXML directly (web/src/axml.ts): it splices out each listed <uses-permission> START+END chunk pair and decrements the root chunk size. AXML chunks reference the string pool by index and each other by order — nothing stores a byte offset — so removal needs no other fixups. Dropping ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION also removes the framework-synthesized background/media-location; dropping the c2dm pair removes push (MIUI's 'send MMS'). The network trio + sensors + storage are KEPT (GameActivity throws SecurityException at startup without ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE — device-confirmed). Matches build/apply_patches.py DROP_PERMS. We do NOT add HIGH_SAMPLING_RATE_SENSORS: register() uses SENSOR_DELAY_GAME (~50Hz, below the 200Hz gate), so it isn't needed.
androidManifest.dropComponentsComment: Tracking/push/ad components, useless offline. Removing the GCM service+receiver+permission kills MIUI's 'Send MMS messages' (it labels cloud-push as MMS); removing the BOOT_COMPLETED receiver kills the 'open windows in background' behavior; AdXAppTracker (ads) and OpenUDID_service (device-ID tracking) are pure telemetry. MIUI's 'installed apps list' and 'clipboard' toggles are NOT manifest-backed (generic AppOps for legacy targetSdk-22 apps) — not removable without a risky targetSdk bump.

addFiles.comment: Optional power feature (NOT needed to 'just play'): the libmod.so ImGui mod menu + its System.loadLibrary('mod') inject into GameActivity.<clinit>. Deferred for the browser patcher.
